cell_legend.rule: Every cell must be pass, or waived with a reason that cites a GOAL.md amendment or recorded constraint. Artifacts are repo-relative paths and must exist.

cells.l0_template: golden byte-equal vs transformers (Gemma3 cases incl. system folding) + EOS/BOS from generation_config
cells.l1_bf16: new-family rule: Mac/CPU per-layer dump vs HF locates zero structural divergence + greedy byte-equal N>=20 on the code-path representative (gemma3-1b)
cells.l2_gguf_metal: GGUF known-answer smoke N>=10 on Metal, semantic answers correct
cells.l2_gptq_cuda: GPTQ known-answer smoke N>=10 on CUDA, semantic answers correct
cells.l3_behavior: smoke ladder mechanics on CUDA serve (stop/max_tokens/stream==non-stream/multi-turn)
cells.l5_concurrency: bench-serve random 256/128 c=1/4/16/32, 100% completion zero errors
cells.perf_vs_llamacpp: same-card same-quant decode throughput >= 0.5x llama.cpp; 0.5-0.8x recorded as known-gap (non-blocking)

models.family: Gemma3 (5:1 SWA, dual rope, GeGLU, sandwich norms)

l0_template.note: chat_template_golden green: single/system/multi_turn/think_history byte-equal (template shared across gemma3 sizes); Gemma has no native tools block — tools cases covered by L4 generic injection

l1_bf16.note: dump compare PASS both CPU and Metal lanes (25/26 layers in bf16 thresholds, logits argmax match); greedy 18/20 byte-equal + 2 tie-flips at HF top1-top2 gap 0.25 (one bf16 ulp, amendment #3 methodology). Metal needed flash_attn acc[8] + gelu_tanh clamp fixes, pinned by gemma3_metal_ops_test

l2_gguf_metal.reason: 27B Q4_K_M (~16.5GB weights) is impractical on the degraded 32GB Mac (W1 precedent: 32B-dense Metal cells, compressor-residue machine state, user-confirmed no reboot). The GGUF lane itself is proven on the same arch: gemma3-1b Q4_K_M Metal known-answer 10/10 with arch-aware tensor mapping, pre-baked (1+w) handling, q_scalar block-rule.
